title: Chocolate Oat Cake
image_alt: Fudgy chocolate oat cake sliced into squares
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease an 8-inch square baking pan.
Blend the rolled oats in a blender or food processor until they resemble flour.
In a large bowl, mash the bananas until smooth. Whisk in the eggs, milk, honey (or maple syrup), and vanilla extract.
Add the blended oats, cocoa powder, baking powder, and salt. Mix until just combined.
Fold in the walnuts and chocolate chips if using.
Pour the batter into the prepared pan and smooth the top.
Bake for 25–30 minutes, until the centre is just set. Don't overbake — it should stay fudgy.
Let cool in the pan for at least 15 minutes before slicing. The cake gets richer as it cools.
